citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
On inspection, a dietary product manufactured by “TestDiet” labelled “P Primate Tab Banana 190mg” was found in the
food storage refrigerator. The product had a documented opened on date of September 13, 2022. Staff did not believe
that the product was not currently being provided to the animals and discarded it on 31 March, 2026. Upon review of the
report of the semiannual facility review dated October 2, 2025, the facility had documented a deficiency regarding the
above-mentioned product found during their inspection with a provided correction date of October 21, 2025, but the
product had not been discarded. Failure to correct observed deficiencies could lead to potential adverse animal welfare
conditions. If program or facility deficiencies are noted, the reports must contain a reasonable and specific plan and
schedule with dates for correcting each deficiency.
Correct from this date forward, 3 April 2026

In an animal room, an ~4.3 square foot enclosure housing a mother and 7 month old infant cynomolgus macaque, one of
the welded bars was no longer in good repair and was bent into the enclosure. Especially with an infant, the missing wire
created dangerous gaps for the size of the animals housed therein. Facility immediately confined the pair to the other side
of the pair of enclosures and subsequently changed out the enclosure. Primary enclosures must be designed and
constructed of suitable materials so that they are structurally sound for the species of nonhuman primates contained in
them and kept in good repair.
Corrected during inspection on 31 March, 2026

In at least two enclosures, two macaques greater than 6 months of age were housed together in a single enclosure
providing ~4.3 feet of space when each animal needs 4.3 feet of floor space. The mother and 7 month old infant were
moved into one enclosure because of the damaged enclosure and another pair were together in an ~4.3 square foot
enclosure. Primary enclosures must meet the minimum space requirements, which is 4.3 square feet for animals between
3-10 kgs. When more than one nonhuman primate is housed in a primary enclosure, the minimum space requirement for
the enclosure is the sum of the minimum floor space required for each individual nonhuman primate housed in the
enclosure.
Corrected during inspection on 31 March, 2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the interim site manager.
Additional Inspectors:
William Campbell, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n

narrative:
2.31(c)(3) 
   
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
On inspection, a dietary product manufactured by “TestDiet” labelled “P Primate Tab Banana 190mg” was found in the 
food storage refrigerator. The product had a documented opened on date of September 13, 2022. Staff did not believe 
that the product was not currently being provided to the animals and discarded it on 31 March, 2026. Upon review of the 
report of the semiannual facility review dated October 2, 2025, the facility had documented a deficiency regarding the 
above-mentioned product found during their inspection with a provided correction date of October 21, 2025, but the 
product had not been discarded. Failure to correct observed deficiencies could lead to potential adverse animal welfare 
conditions. If program or facility deficiencies are noted, the reports must contain a reasonable and specific plan and 
schedule with dates for correcting each deficiency.  
 
 Correct from this date forward, 3 April 2026 
3.80(a)(1) 
   
Primary enclosures. 
In an animal room, an ~4.3 square foot enclosure housing a mother and 7 month old infant cynomolgus macaque, one of 
the welded bars was no longer in good repair and was bent into the enclosure. Especially with an infant, the missing wire 
created dangerous gaps for the size of the animals housed therein. Facility immediately confined the pair to the other side 
of the pair of enclosures and subsequently changed out the enclosure. Primary enclosures must be designed and 
constructed of suitable materials so that they are structurally sound for the species of nonhuman primates contained in 
them and kept in good repair. 
 
Corrected during inspection on 31 March, 2026 
3.80(b)(4) 
   
Primary enclosures. 
In at least two enclosures, two macaques greater than 6 months of age were housed together in a single enclosure 
providing ~4.3 feet of space when each animal needs 4.3 feet of floor space. The mother and 7 month old infant were 
moved into one enclosure because of the damaged enclosure and another pair were together in an ~4.3 square foot

enclosure. Primary enclosures must meet the minimum space requirements, which is 4.3 square feet for animals between 
3-10 kgs. When more than one nonhuman primate is housed in a primary enclosure, the minimum space requirement for 
the enclosure is the sum of the minimum floor space required for each individual nonhuman primate housed in the 
enclosure. 
 
Corrected during inspection on 31 March, 2026 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the interim site manager. 
 
Additional Inspectors: 
William Campbell, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n
